blocks.normalized_text: senator-judge padilla expressed concern over the developments related to the impeachment court questioning whether the same were merely coincidental citing as examples the detention of senator-judges estrada and marcoleta and the ombudsman s reported actions involving himself and senator-judge cayetano a he noted that reports of an investigation into matters related to the southeast asian sea games surfaced a day after senator-judge cayetano a questioned the bangkok trip of national bureau of investigation nbi director melvin matibag he asked whether the senator-judges should be concerned about such developments emphasizing that the impeachment court as an independent constitutional body must be allowed to conduct its proceedings free from undue interference
blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Padilla expressed concern over the developments related to the Impeachment Court, questioning whether the same were merely coincidental, citing as examples, the detention of Senator-Judges Estrada and Marcoleta, and the Ombudsman’s reported actions involving himself and Senator-Judge Cayetano (A). He noted that reports of an investigation into matters related to the Southeast Asian (SEA) Games surfaced a day after Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) questioned the Bangkok trip of National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) Director Melvin Matibag. He asked whether the senator-judges should be concerned about such developments, emphasizing that the Impeachment Court, as an independent constitutional body, must be allowed to conduct its proceedings free from undue interference.

Senator-Judge Padilla expressed concern over the developments related to the Impeachment 
Court, questioning whether the same were merely coincidental, citing as examples, the detention of 
Senator-Judges Estrada and Marcoleta, and the Ombudsman’s reported actions involving himself 
and Senator-Judge Cayetano (A). He noted that reports of an investigation into matters related to 
the Southeast Asian (SEA) Games surfaced a day after Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) questioned 
the Bangkok trip of National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) Director Melvin Matibag. He asked 
whether the senator-judges should be concerned about such developments, emphasizing that the 
Impeachment Court, as an independent constitutional body, must be allowed to conduct its 
proceedings free from undue interference.

blocks.normalized_text: senator-judge cayetano a averred that the nbi s recently announced investigation on the alleged anomalies involving the 2019 sea games appeared to pertain to him as chair of the event s organizing committee he added that the announcement was made a day after he questioned the nbi director s trip to bangkok with his supposed appearance as a witness for the prosecution he said that if the intention was to create a chilling effect on the impeachment proceedings he believed that the senate already felt such chilling effect since may 11 2026 when nbi agents pursued senator-judge dela rosa in the senate building and may 13 2026 when there was armed confrontation between nbi agents and personnel of the senate office of sergeant-at-arms osaa within the senate premises he also recalled that director matibag had failed to fulfill his commitments to produce the alleged warrant of the international criminal court icc for the arrest of senator dela rosa discipline the agents involved in the assault of an osaa personnel and implement the senate s contempt order
blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) averred that the NBI’s recently announced investigation on the alleged anomalies involving the 2019 SEA Games appeared to pertain to him as chair of the event’s organizing committee. He added that the announcement was made a day after he questioned the NBI Director’s trip to Bangkok with his supposed appearance as a witness for the Prosecution He said that if the intention was to create a chilling effect on the impeachment proceedings, he believed that the Senate already felt such chilling effect since May 11, 2026 when NBI agents pursued Senator-Judge Dela Rosa in the Senate building and May 13, 2026 when there was armed confrontation between NBI agents and personnel of the Senate Office of Sergeant-At-Arms (OSAA) within the Senate premises. He also recalled that Director Matibag had failed to fulfill his commitments to produce the alleged warrant of the International Criminal Court (ICC) for the arrest of Senator Dela Rosa, discipline the agents involved in the assault of an OSAA personnel, and implement the Senate’s contempt order.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) averred that the NBI’s recently announced investigation 
on the alleged anomalies involving the 2019 SEA Games appeared to pertain to him as chair 
of the event’s organizing committee. He added that the announcement was made a day after 
he questioned the NBI Director’s trip to Bangkok with his supposed appearance as a witness 
for the Prosecution
He said that if the intention was to create a chilling effect on the impeachment proceedings, 
he believed that the Senate already felt such chilling effect since May 11, 2026 when NBI agents 
pursued Senator-Judge Dela Rosa in the Senate building and May 13, 2026 when there was armed 
confrontation between NBI agents and personnel of the Senate Office of Sergeant-At-Arms 
(OSAA) within the Senate premises. He also recalled that Director Matibag had failed to fulfill 
his commitments to produce the alleged warrant of the International Criminal Court (ICC) for the 
arrest of Senator Dela Rosa, discipline the agents involved in the assault of an OSAA personnel, 
and implement the Senate’s contempt order.

blocks.normalized_text: senator-judge cayetano a maintained that the allegations of director matibag were misplaced explaining that the absence of public bidding does not by itself indicate an anomaly as the procurement law recognizes alternative modes of procurement he likewise denied participation in the alleged congressional insertion of funds for the questioned infrastructure projects stating that he was not a member of either house of congress at that time he added that the projects were implemented by the bases conversion and development auhtority bcda then headed by close advisers of the incumbent president and that the contractor under scrutiny was the same contractor undertaking the construction of the pasig city hall
blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) maintained that the allegations of Director Matibag were misplaced, explaining that the absence of public bidding does not, by itself, indicate an anomaly as the procurement law recognizes alternative modes of procurement. He likewise denied participation in the alleged congressional insertion of funds for the questioned infrastructure projects, stating that he was not a Member of either House of Congress at that time. He added that the projects were implemented by the Bases Conversion and Development Auhtority (BCDA), then headed by close advisers of the incumbent President, and that the contractor under scrutiny was the same contractor undertaking the construction of the Pasig City Hall.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) maintained that the allegations of Director Matibag were 
misplaced, explaining that the absence of public bidding does not, by itself, indicate an anomaly as 
the procurement law recognizes alternative modes of procurement. He likewise denied participation 
in the alleged congressional insertion of funds for the questioned infrastructure projects, stating that 
he was not a Member of either House of Congress at that time. He added that the projects were 
implemented by the Bases Conversion and Development Auhtority (BCDA), then headed by close 
advisers of the incumbent President, and that the contractor under scrutiny was the same contractor 
undertaking the construction of the Pasig City Hall.

Ay on sa report ng Anti-Money Laundering Council na isinumite sa Committee on Justice ng 
House of Representatives, the financial activity of Vice President Sara Z. Duterte increased 
significantly starting 2007 when transaction value surged to P208.15 million. According to this 
report, which is marked as Exhibit P-II-7-8,1 quote, “Financial movements intensified between 2009 
and 2013, with consistently high totals exceeding P400 million, including notable peaks in 2009 
(P704.93 million), 2010 (P648.58 million), and 2011 (P597.15 milUon).” Si VP Sara po ay nanungkulan 
bilang Vice Mayor ng Davao City mula 2007 hanggang 2010. Naglingkod naman siya bilang 
Mayor ng Davao City mula 2010 hanggang 2013. From 2007 to 2013, ayon sa records ng AMLC, 
lumampas sa P3 billion ang kaniyang financial activity.

From what I have gathered and read, Your Honors, please, this is not the first time this Court 
has been confronted with this issue. Sa impeachment ni dating Chief Justice Renato Corona, 
tinanggap ng Hukumang ito ang mga ebidensiyang nakalap bago pa man siya naupo bilang 
Punong Hukom. I believe it was then-Senator-Judge, now Presiding Officer, Francis Escudero, who 
raised the question: “Will the Impeachment Court consider only Corona’s acts as Chief Justice?” 
This, after observing that some exhibits predated his appointment as Chief Justice. This matter was 
taken up in caucus, after which this Court decided to allow such evidence.

blocks.raw_text: When Philippine Savings Bank President Pascual Garcia III testified before this Court in the same Corona trial, he refused to answer the question of then- Senator-Judge Franklin Drilon on the starting balance of Corona’s two-peso-denominated accounts. But if I’m not mistaken, it was Senator-Judge Alan Peter Cayetano who said that records of initial deposits are among the “opening documents” that should have been brought to the Impeachment Court. During the same hearing, the Presiding Officer ordered the issuance of a subpoena to PSBank Katipunan Branch Manager Anabelle Tiongson to bring all the bank records of the Chief Justice. blocks.source_text:
When Philippine Savings Bank President Pascual Garcia III testified before this Court in the 
same Corona trial, he refused to answer the question of then- Senator-Judge Franklin Drilon on the 
starting balance of Corona’s two-peso-denominated accounts. But if I’m not mistaken, it was 
Senator-Judge Alan Peter Cayetano who said that records of initial deposits are among the 
“opening documents” that should have been brought to the Impeachment Court. After reading the 
subpoena. Presiding Officer Senator Juan Ponce Emile held that Senator-Judge Cayetano’s 
observation was correct. During the same hearing, the Presiding Officer ordered the issuance of 
a subpoena to PSBank Katipunan Branch Manager Anabelle Tiongson to bring all the bank records 
of the Chief Justice. A few days later, the Impeachment Court denied the Defense’s motion to quash 
the subpoena for the peso bank accounts of CJ Corona. The Presiding Officer explained that the 
purpose of the subpoena is to find out if Corona has not included the deposits in his SALN. This 
Court also denied the Defense’s motion to suppress evidence on Corona’s bank accounts.

Impeachment is primarily a legal and constitutional procedure with political characteristics. It 
is sui generis, but it is not a purely political proceeding. Ibig sabihin po noon, the Bill of Rights, 
especially the due process clause, applies in all stages of the impeachment process. Your Honors, 
those are not my words—those are the words of the Supreme Court in Duterte v. House of 
Representatives, and that is the law of the land. When we speak of due process, we offer a simple 
proposition that evidence must be present to support each and every accusation. Pero hindi po 
iyon iyong nangyari dito. Balikan po natin kung saan tayo, kung saan nagsimula itong 
proceeding na ito.

Now, Your Honors, let’s go to the Bank Secrecy Law, RA 1405. The State policy has always 
been to favor the secrecy of bank deposits. Your Honors. Dito, while we recognize that 
impeachment is, in fact, an exception. Your Honors, we submit that this exception is not automatic. 
Meaning, hindi porket may exception for impeachment, puwede na kaagad natin buksan ang 
lahat ng bank records na hinihingi ng Prosecution. Why? Because, Your Honors, this exception, 
does not dispense with constitutional safeguards. It does not abolish the right to due process. So 
dapat po, meron pa rin tayong basehan. If you look at the Articles of Impeachment, wala naman 
po doon na nagsasabing— ‘yung sinasabing unexplained wealth coming from—even if it was an 
illegal activity ay pinasok sa isang specific account. In fact. Your Honors, this exception does not 
automatically mean that we should permit a fishing expedition. Iyon nga pong mga bank account 
numbers na nafaz-specify sa request for the issuance of a subpoena, ang aming posisyon po diyan, 
iyan po ay nakalap doon sa illegal disclosure, with all due respect, na ginawa ng AMLC before 
the Committee on Justice.

Let me address each of the points that the defense has raised in their oral argument. Sabi po 
nila, ma ay due process daw ang isang paulit-ulit na dinidiin ng defense. But there is no debate 
that due process applies in this proceeding, just like in any other proceeding. The real issue before 
this Honorable Court is, “will the subpoenas that we are asking be issued by this Court actually 
deprive the Respondent of any form of due process?” Ang anting paniwala po ay hindi. And I’ll— 
allow me to explain why.

The Presiding Officer stated that the senator-judges, in caucus, agreed to defer action on the 
Prosecution’s request for the issuance of subpoenas duces tecum covering the BIR, AMLC, and 
bank records of Vice President Duterte and her husband until Monday, July 20, 2026, to afford 
senator-judges more time to study the memoranda submitted by the Counsel for the Prosecution 
and the Counsel for Respondent, together with the points raised therein. He added that the senator- 
judges wishing to propound questions to either party would be allotted 10 minutes each, subject to 
an aggregate limit of one hour per side during the plenary debate.

Citing redundancy, surplusage, and lack of necessity. Representative Luistro likewise manifested 
that the Prosecution would no longer be presenting the following witnesses for Article IV for the 
reasons stated below:

threat seriously, as demonstrated by the conduct of a full-scale investigation and the 
recommendation to file a criminal complaint.

blocks.normalized_text: attorney sison asserted that as impeachment is a constitutional process the proceedings must remain fair and balanced she argued that manifestations containing assertions laden with conclusions or one-sided advocacy by either party would not aid the court in determining the respondent s guilt or innocence under the articles of impeachment she further clarified that her position was based on substantive constitutional principles rather than technical rules of evidence the presiding officer took note of attorney sison s manifestation
blocks.raw_text: Attorney Sison asserted that as impeachment is a constitutional process, the proceedings must remain fair and balanced. She argued that manifestations containing assertions laden with conclusions or one-sided advocacy by either party would not aid the Court in determining the respondent’s guilt or innocence under the Articles of Impeachment. She further clarified that her position was based on substantive constitutional principles, rather than technical rules of evidence. The Presiding Officer took note of Attorney Sison’s manifestation.
blocks.source_text:
Attorney Sison asserted that as impeachment is a constitutional process, the proceedings must 
remain fair and balanced. She argued that manifestations containing assertions laden with 
conclusions or one-sided advocacy by either party would not aid the Court in determining the 
respondent’s guilt or innocence under the Articles of Impeachment. She further clarified that her 
position was based on substantive constitutional principles, rather than technical rules of evidence. 
The Presiding Officer took note of Attorney Sison’s manifestation.

In support of the Presiding Officer’s ruling. Senator-Judge Cayetano (P) reminded counsel for 
both parties to refrain from lengthy explanations or personal observations when withdrawing 
witnesses, as these may convey conclusions or factual assertions that should be borne out witness 
examination. She pointed out that the rule is not intended to impose unnecessary technical rules, 
but to ensure that counsel confine themselves to asking questions while witnesses provide the 
testimony.
